old_claim.note: crispr_gene_editing was previously treated as a plausible hard prerequisite for ex_vivo_crispr_cell_therapy without a field-specific edge receipt.

new_claim.note: Ex vivo CRISPR cell therapy depends on CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing of patient-derived cells before reinfusion.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and clinical method summary describing CRISPR-Cas9 editing of patient hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells before infusion.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The clinical report describes ex vivo treatment by editing patient-derived cells with CRISPR-Cas9 and returning the edited cells as the therapeutic product.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This is a method dependency rather than a loose historical relationship because the therapy workflow is defined by performing CRISPR editing outside the body.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: ex_vivo_crispr_cell_therapy -> crispr_gene_editing remains required; this receipt adds source-shaped evidence for the existing CRISPR field edge.
would_reject_if: The source described only gene therapy without CRISPR-Cas9 editing.
The edge type was changed from required without preserving the editing workflow dependency elsewhere.
The receipt relied only on FDA approval status rather than the therapeutic method.
short_reason: Ex vivo CRISPR cell therapy is operationally built around CRISPR-Cas9 editing of removed cells.
